clay_relevance: Closes the concentrated Hou-Luo branch inside the axisymmetric symmetry class, not the full Clay problem.
closure_condition: A uniform PDE comparison rules out finite-time BKM blowup for all beta>0 axisymmetric-with-swirl data, including large Re.
existing_support: u1 has an exact maximum principle and no autonomous source
localized beta>0 comparison gives viscous/source divergence
Sprint132 isolates large-Re axisymmetric data as an open gate
key_estimate_or_claim: For beta>0 and nu>0, axisymmetric swirl source growth cannot outrun localized diffusion uniformly over large Reynolds data.
proof_objective: Upgrade the moderate-Re Hou-Luo viscous obstruction to a full PDE estimate that prevents a large-Re transient from reaching BKM divergence before viscosity dominates.

clay_relevance: Closes the non-concentrating axisymmetric boundary case only.
closure_condition: A beta=0 continuation estimate shows the marginal source term cannot force BKM divergence from finite-energy smooth data.
existing_support: the source remains exactly partial_z(u1^2)=2 u1 partial_z u1
u1 remains bounded by its initial Linf norm for nu>0
Sprint132 names beta=0 as a separate open marginal gate
key_estimate_or_claim: Large-scale shear without shrinking delta cannot sustain BKM growth unless a new nonlocal axisymmetric amplification estimate is available.
proof_objective: Analyze the no-concentration boundary regime where beta=0 and the Sprint130/131 viscous dominance ratio no longer diverges.

clay_relevance: Provides a genuine subcase theorem for Clay without claiming universal 3D exhaustion.
closure_condition: Every axisymmetric-with-swirl blowup candidate is forced into one of the already closed gates G1 or G2.
existing_support: Sprint132 shows full 3D mechanism exhaustion is structurally blocked
axisymmetric symmetry removes non-axisymmetric WBF and triad lanes
the target has finite gates rather than open-ended route enumeration
key_estimate_or_claim: Within the axisymmetric-with-swirl class, potential singular sequences admit a finite scaling dichotomy: concentrated beta>0 or marginal beta=0.
proof_objective: Replace full 3D mechanism enumeration with an axisymmetric structure theorem showing every axisymmetric BKM-divergent scenario enters either beta>0 concentration or beta=0 marginal scaling after suitable localization.

clay_relevance: Would be a major Clay-adjacent subcase, still strictly weaker than full 3D Navier-Stokes regularity.
closure_condition: G1, G2, and G3 are closed with compatible hypotheses and no remaining large-data axisymmetric exception.
existing_support: local smooth continuation is governed by BKM-type criteria
the axisymmetric swirl PDE has exact u1 and omega1 equations
Sprint130-132 surfaces provide named support for the assembly map
key_estimate_or_claim: No axisymmetric-with-swirl solution can realize finite-time BKM divergence once the large-Re, marginal, and exhaustion gates are closed.
proof_objective: Assemble gates G1-G3 with standard local existence and BKM continuation into global regularity for axisymmetric NS with swirl.
